step_by_step_thinking:
Endocarditis of RHD mainly involves the valve cusps where inflammation leads to changes such as irregular thickness and restricted movement. Therefore, the site of lesion in endocarditis of RHD would likely involve the areas near the valve cusps rather than along the lines of closure, on both sides of the valve, or just the free margins.
